rows.title: A "2011: Cannot Log into Database." Error Occurs When Trying to Configure WebLink Through the Web Products Administrator's Utility.
rows.detailTitle: A "2011: Cannot Log into Database." Error Occurs When Trying to Configure WebLink Through the Web Products Administrator's Utility.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "2011: Cannot Log into Database." Error Occurs When Trying to Configure WebLink Through the Web Products Administrator's Utility. Product: WebLink 6 KB: 1000622 Published: June 28, 2004 Last Updated: June 28, 2004 Summary When configuring WebLink with the Web Products Administrator's Utility after installing WebLink, you may receive the following error message: 2011: Cannot log into database. Note: Earlier versions of WebLink may contain the WebLink Administrator's Utility instead of the Web Products Administrator's Utility. Cause There are two situations where this error can arise. The first situation is when the LFWEBLINK.ini file is not properly configured. This error can also occur because there is a conflict with your license file. The specific causes are listed as follows: The [Database] section of the LFWEBLINK.ini file contains incorrect settings. See Resolution 1. There is no license file in the WebLink installation directory. See Resolution 2. Resolution 1 The [Database] section of the LFWEBLINK.ini file must be modifiied to a state that is valid for use with WebLink. There should be no punctuation marks and the "/weblink" section must be in all lower case. The following sample is the correct format of a LFWEBLINK.ini [Database] section: [Database] Server=ComputerName\LaserFiche /weblink=LaserFiche Note: If you are using LaserFiche United (Standard\Enterprise), then you should only specify the name of the computer where the SQL server resides. Resolution 2 Open the WebLink installation folder. The default location is C:\Program Files\LaserFiche\WebLink. The folder must contain a LF.lic license file. If there is no license file, please contact your certified dealer to obtain a license. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "The page cannot be found" Error Occurs When Printing To PDF With WebLink 8. 15 years ago be-found-quot-error-occurs-when-printing-to-pdf-with-weblink-8- WebLink 8 Summary When you click the Print PDF button in the WebLink document viewer, you may receive ... may displ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012804/a-the-page-cannot-be-found ...
rows.title: A "The page cannot be found" Error Occurs When Printing To PDF With WebLink 8.
rows.detailTitle: A "The page cannot be found" Error Occurs When Printing To PDF With WebLink 8.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "The page cannot be found" Error Occurs When Printing To PDF With WebLink 8. Product: WebLink 8 KB: 1012804 Published: June 13, 2011 Last Updated: June 13, 2011 Summary When you click the Print PDF button in the WebLink document viewer, you may receive an HTTP 404 error. Different browsers may display variations of the following error messages: The page cannot be found. The webpage cannot be found. The resource you are looking for has been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable. Cause This issue can occur if the WebLink 8 virtual directory is missing a custom handler mapping for .pdf extensions. The WebLink 8 installation process will create the necessary handler mapping for the default WebLink 8 virtual directory; however, you must manually create the mapping if you decide to create a new virtual directory. Resolution Use the Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager to create a handler mapping. IIS 7 on Windows Server 2008 Click Start, point to Administrative Tools, and click Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager. Expand the Default Web Site and select the appropriate WebLink 8 virtual directory. In the Features View, double-click Handler Mappings. In the Actions pane, click on Add Script Map.... In the Add Script Map dialog box, in the Request path text box, type: *.pdf In the Executable text box, specify: C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v2.0.50727\aspnet_isapi.dll In the Name text box, specify: WebLinkPdfHandler Click OK to close the Add Script Map dialog box and save your changes. Click Yes if you are prompted to confirm your action. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailTitle: Digital Signatures are Not Working in Laserfiche Windows Client and You May Receive the Following Error Message: "No usable certificates found. To import a certificate, use the Certificate Manager or contact your system administrator."
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Digital Signatures are Not Working in Laserfiche Windows Client and You May Receive the Following Error Message: "No usable certificates found. To import a certificate, use the Certificate Manager or contact your system administrator." Product: Laserfiche Client 10 KB: 1013913 Published: May 7, 2020 Last Updated: May 11, 2020 Summary You may find that you cannot add a digital signature to a document in Laserfiche Windows client. You may also receive the following message within the Select Certificate dialog box: "No usable certificates found. To import a certificate, use the Certificate Manager or contact your system administrator." Cause One potential cause is that there is no valid certificate under the current user's personal certificate store. Resolution Verify that there is a valid certificate in the user's personal certificate store. Ensure that the certificate is installed with the private key. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 3
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 3 Product: Import Agent KB: 1014306 Published: June 29, 2021 Last Updated: December 13, 2022 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 3. This update has been superseded by KB 1014408 Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 7. Fixes for Import Agent and Email Archive When importing TIF files via XML wtih the [Settings]ImportJPEGCompressionLevel parameter set, the imported file's size is no longer larger than the original file. (309054) Import Agent now successfully generates Laserfiche pages properly on PDFs with stamps. (325358) Page generation in Import Agent no longer loses all text from PDF Version 1.4 files produced by Skia m81-m83. (298762) Importing PDF documents no longer adds a black mark to pages when "Generate Laserfiche pages" is enable. (325312) You can now generate pages on documents updated from DocuSign. (301202) When creating an Import Agent or Email Archive profile, the repository login screen now refers to TLS instead of SSL. (275050) In Email Archive, an unhandled error no longer occurs when a field has been assigned an email-related token and its header is missing. (324676) An error no longer occours when importing an email without a "From" property. (277628) The %(EmailUniqueMessageID) token now returns a unique value. (321443) The update includes the following files for Import Agent: ImportAgentConfig.exe (10.4.0.186) ImportAgentService.exe (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\ar\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\es\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\fr\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\it\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\pt-BR\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\zh-Hans\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Import Agent\zh-Hant\ImportAgentConfig.resources.dll (10.4.0.186) Laserfiche.PdfServices.dll (10.4.3.8) Laserfiche.PdfServices.Compression.dll (10.4.3.8) O2S.Components.PDFRender4NET.Win.dll (6.1.2.11) O2S.Components.PDFView4NET.Win.dll (6.1.2.11) The update includes the following files for Email Archive: Laserfiche.EmailArchive.exe (10.4.0.129) Laserfiche.EmailArchive.ConfigUtility.exe (10.4.0.129) Laserfiche.S22.Imap.dll (10.4.0129) Laserfiche.PdfServices.dll (10.4.3.8) Laserfiche.PdfServices.Compression.dll (10.4.3.8) O2S.Components.PDFRender4NET.Win.dll (6.1.2.11) O2S.Components.PDFView4NET.Win.dll (6.1.2.11) Resolution To update Import Agent: Click the following link to download the update: KB1014306.zip On the computer hosting Import Agent, extract and run the .exe file. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche 10.2 7 years ago a named device would receive a "Cannot connect to the Laserfiche Directory Server. [9528]" error. This issue has been resolved. (152768) A "User name or password incorrect... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013820/list-of-changes-for-las ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche 10.2
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Laserfiche 10.2 Product: Laserfiche 10.2 KB: 1013820 Published: January 11, 2017 Last Updated: August 7, 2018 Summary Laserfiche 10.2 includes many new features, as well as stability and performance enhancements. Laserfiche 10.2 includes many new features and enhancements across the Laserfiche suite that allow you to work with your repository and your information more easily and efficiently. This release also introduces Laserfiche Federated Search, a new search site for Laserfiche Rio that allows you to search across multiple repositories. In addition, Laserfiche 10.2 includes stability and performance enhancements. New Features in the Laserfiche Server, Web Client, and Windows Client Multi-Value Field Groups In some cases, values across multiple fields on a single document may be related to one another. For example, on an invoice, you might have multiple items, each with its own name, product code, price, and amount purchased. On a report, you may have multiple authors, each of whom has an email address associated with them. While multi-value fields allow you to store multiple values, it can be difficult to tell at a glance which price corresponds to which item, or which author has which email address. Multi-value field groups solve this problem by allowing you to specify that multiple multi-value fields are related. You could associate the "Product Name," "Product Code," "Price," and "Amount Purchased" multi-value fields as all belonging to the same group within the "Invoice" template. Then, when a user opens a document with that template, they will be able to see which name, code, price, and amount purchased values go together, and to add or modify more invoice items appropriately. Configurable Search Thesaurus In some cases, users may have difficulty finding documents because they are using search terms synonymous in the documents that they are looking for, but not terms actually in those documents. A user might search for a document using a common acronym when the document text spells out the word, or vice versa, or they might use a general term when the document uses more industry-specific jargon, or a nickname or set of initials when a full name was used in the document. While features like root word search and fuzzy search can help with this issue, they may be unable to solve it if the two terms are sufficiently different, or specific to a particular industry or company. The new configurable search thesaurus allows administrators to define associations between words. When one word is searched, both it and its synonyms will be returned. This association can go both ways (so that, for example, searching for "LF" returns documents with the word "Laserfiche" and vice versa), or only one way (so that searching for "Laserfiche" returns documents with the word "Workflow," but not vice versa). In the Laserfiche Web Administration Console, administrators can set up custom thesaurus entries specific to their company or industry, and in addition, they can import and customize a predefined list of many common synonyms. New Search Syntax Search Types The Laserfiche Server now supports several new search types that can be performed using the search syntax. Attachment Annotation Text: The attachment annotation text search allows you to perform full-text searches of attachment annotations and return documents whose attachment annotations contain the specified search terms. Parent Template Name: The parent template name search allows you to locate documents based on the template assigned to their parent folder. All immediate children of folders with the specified template will be returned, although the folder itself and documents further down the folder tree will not (unless they also are in a folder with the specified template). Security: Laserfiche now supports searching for entries based on what trustees have entry access rights assigned to them for that entry, the specific entry access rights involved, whether the right is allowed or denied, and the scope of that right. For example, you could use this search to determine on which documents Mary has had the Delete entry access right granted. This search only applies to rights directly set on the document, and does not take inheritance into account. Other New Features Streamlined Entry Access Rights: To allow you to modify your entry access rights more quickly and easily, the Laserfiche web and Windows clients now group related entry access rights together. This allows you to quickly grant or deny access to groups of related rights (such as Create Documents, Create Folders, and Append Data) without having to select each one individually. You can also expand these rights groups and modify each right within it individually if you want to apply them in a more granular fashion. Password Updates: Users who sign in using Laserfiche accounts managed through Laserfiche Directory Server can now change their passwords direct

rows.snippet: Import Agent 8 May Not Send Documents to Laserfiche When Laserfiche Server 8 Is Not Using The Default HTTP Listening Port. 15 years ago Import Agent 8 May Not Send Documents to Laserfiche When Laserfiche Server 8 Is Not Using The Default HTTP Listening Port. February 15, 2011 | KB: 1012750 https://support.laser... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012750/import-agent-8-may-not-sen ...
rows.title: Import Agent 8 May Not Send Documents to Laserfiche When Laserfiche Server 8 Is Not Using The Default HTTP Listening Port.
rows.detailTitle: Import Agent 8 May Not Send Documents to Laserfiche When Laserfiche Server 8 Is Not Using The Default HTTP Listening Port.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Import Agent 8 May Not Send Documents to Laserfiche When Laserfiche Server 8 Is Not Using The Default HTTP Listening Port. Product: Import Agent 8 KB: 1012750 Published: February 14, 2011 Last Updated: February 15, 2011 Summary The Import Agent Service may display one of the following error messages in the Windows Event Log when the Laserfiche Server 8 is not using the default HTTP listening port (80 or 5050): There was an error executing the profile ProfileName. (There was an error logging into the repository. - Could not connect to the Laserfiche Server.) There was an error executing the profile ProfileName. (There was an error logging into the repository. - No response received from the server.) When loading the Import Agent Configuration Utility, you are prompted to specify your repository information. Even after you specify the server name as YourLFServer:Port, the profile still does not successfully run. Cause The configuration utility does not properly write the server string with the custom port value to the registry. Workaround Manually modify Server string value under the desired "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Laserfiche\Import Agent 8\Profiles\YourProfileName" registry key to reflect the appropriate HTTP port for your Laserfiche Server. Note: If the Import Agent service is installed on a 64-bit version of Windows, the Server string value is located under the "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Laserfiche\Import Agent 8\Profiles\YourProfileName" registry key. Important: Incorrectly editing the registry may adversely affect your system. Before you edit the registry, make sure you understand how to restore it if a problem occurs. To modify the Server string value On the computer hosting Import Agent, click Start, All Programs, Accessories, and click Command Prompt to load a command prompt. Note: In a UAC environment, load an elevated command prompt by right-clicking on Command Prompt and select Run as administrator. Type the following and press ENTER to load the Windows Registry Editor: regedit Expand HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE. Expand SOFTWARE. Expand Laserfiche. Expand Import Agent 8. Expand Profiles. Expand YourProfileName. Double-click the Server string value and change the value to the following format: YourLFServer:Port Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Laserfiche Connector 9 Closes Immediately After Starting. 9 years ago kb/1013746/laserfiche-connector-9-closes-immediately-after-starting- Laserfiche Connector 9, Laserfiche Connector 10 Summary Laserfiche Connector 9 may close immediately af... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013746/laserfiche-connector-9-clo ...
rows.title: Laserfiche Connector 9 Closes Immediately After Starting.
rows.detailTitle: Laserfiche Connector 9 Closes Immediately After Starting.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Laserfiche Connector 9 Closes Immediately After Starting. Product: Laserfiche Connector 9, Laserfiche Connector 10 KB: 1013746 Published: September 12, 2016 Last Updated: September 12, 2016 Summary Laserfiche Connector 9 may close immediately after starting. However, Laserfiche Connector starts successfully if started by a different user account on the same computer. Cause This issue can occur when there is an invalid configuration file and Laserfiche Connector cannot correctly read the configuration file. Workaround Manually delete the invalid configuration files for the affected user account. Browse to the following folder: C:\Users\SampleUserName\AppData\Roaming\Laserfiche\Connector\OverrideSettings Delete the files in the folder. As the affected user, start Laserfiche Connector. Note: Be aware that the AppData folder for users is a hidden folder by default. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Laserfiche 9 Compatibility Information for Windows 10, Windows Server 2016 and Windows Server 2019. 5 years ago for-windows-10-windows-server-2016-and-windows-server-2019- Connector 9, Forms 9, Laserfiche 9, Web Access 9, WebLink 9 Summary Laserfiche Suite products version 9 can be installed ... will dis... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013672/laserfiche-9-compatibility-inform ...
rows.title: Laserfiche 9 Compatibility Information for Windows 10, Windows Server 2016 and Windows Server 2019.
rows.detailTitle: Laserfiche 9 Compatibility Information for Windows 10, Windows Server 2016 and Windows Server 2019.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Laserfiche 9 Compatibility Information for Windows 10, Windows Server 2016 and Windows Server 2019. Product: Connector 9, Forms 9, Laserfiche 9, Web Access 9, WebLink 9 KB: 1013672 Published: July 29, 2015 Last Updated: March 3, 2021 Summary Laserfiche Suite products version 9 can be installed on Windows 10/Windows Server 2016/Windows Server 2019 and work as expected for most situations. See below for specific compatibility issues regarding Microsoft Edge and IIS 10. Microsoft HTML Edge Compatibility Microsoft HTML Edge does not yet offer extension/plugin support. This affects Web Access's ability to detect whether Scanning or the Office Integration are already installed on your computer. In addition, you will not be able to sign documents through Web Access. Similarly, the Workflow Web website will be unable to directly open the currently displayed workflow definition in the Workflow Designer. If you require these features, please use other supported Web browsers. Certain icons in Laserfiche Forms may not display. Microsoft HTML Edge will display a blank area for the affected icons. Laserfiche Connector may not be able to detect controls using the JavaScript method. If you cannot select the controls you want with JavaScript, try the UI automation method or use OCR to extract text. IIS 10 Compatibility Laserfiche Web 9.x applications are compatible with IIS 10 itself, but the existing installations may not be able to properly detect that IIS 10 is enabled on Windows 10/Windows Server 2016. This can prevent the pre-requisite steps in the installation packages from completing successfully. This issue has been remedied in version 10. As a temporary workaround, manually modify the registry and change a version number that the installation packages use to detect installed IIS version. Open the Windows Registry Editor. On the 32-bit and 64-bit versions of the aforementioned Windows operating systems, browse to the following key: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\InetStp Look for the DWORD value: MajorVersion Change the decimal value from 10 to 8. On the 64-bit version of Windows, repeat the above steps with the following key: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\InetStp Run the desired Laserfiche Web application installation. Set the decimal value of MajorVersion back to 10. Note: This IIS detection issue does not affect the WebLink 9 Service Pack 1 or Workflow installation packages. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: A "Failed to load annotations. The image cannot be modified. General database error. [9008]" Error May Occur In Laserfiche 9.1 When Opening Documents That Contain Annotations.
rows.detailTitle: A "Failed to load annotations. The image cannot be modified. General database error. [9008]" Error May Occur In Laserfiche 9.1 When Opening Documents That Contain Annotations.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "Failed to load annotations. The image cannot be modified. General database error. [9008]" Error May Occur In Laserfiche 9.1 When Opening Documents That Contain Annotations. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1013410 Published: January 27, 2014 Last Updated: March 19, 2014 Summary In certain Laserfiche 9.1 repositories that were previously upgraded from Laserfiche 7, you may receive the following error when opening a document that contains annotations: Failed to load annotations. The image cannot be modified. General database error. [9008] Cause In Laserfiche 7, Laserfiche did not store the creator of an annotation. During the initial Laserfiche 7 to Laserfiche 8/9 database migration process, the Migration Utility may improperly create the placeholder value. Resolution Use the following SQL script to trim the offending fields. Stop the Laserfiche Server service before running the script. Warning: Before modifying your Laserfiche database, make sure that you have a valid backup and understand how to restore the database if a problem occurs. Download the following zip file containing the .sql script file. KB1013410.zip The script contains the following SQL statements. DECLARE @tblname sysname DECLARE @colname sysname DECLARE @stmt nvarchar(500) DECLARE sidcol_cursor CURSOR FOR SELECT c.TABLE_NAME, COLUMN_NAME FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS c JOIN INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES t ON c.TABLE_NAME = t.TABLE_NAME WHERE DATA_TYPE = ''varbinary'' AND CHARACTER_OCTET_LENGTH = 85 AND t.TABLE_TYPE = ''BASE TABLE'' OPEN sidcol_cursor FETCH NEXT FROM sidcol_cursor INTO @tblname, @colname WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 BEGIN SET @stmt = ''UPDATE '' + @tblname + '' SET '' + @colname + '' = SUBSTRING('' + @colname + '', 1, 68)'' EXEC sp_executesql @stmt PRINT @stmt FETCH NEXT FROM sidcol_cursor INTO @tblname, @colname END CLOSE sidcol_cursor DEALLOCATE sidcol_cursor GO Note: The sample SQL listed above will modify all columns in the database of type varbinary(85). Be aware that if you have added custom tables with columns of this type to the Laserfiche database that they will also be modified. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: A "7020: Cannot open this version of the database." Error Occurs When Logging into a Database
rows.detailTitle: A "7020: Cannot open this version of the database." Error Occurs When Logging into a Database
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "7020: Cannot open this version of the database." Error Occurs When Logging into a Database Product: Laserfiche 6, Plus 6 KB: 1000298 Published: March 23, 2004 Last Updated: May 12, 2014 Summary When you log into the database, you may receive the following error message: 7020: Cannot open this version of the database Cause The LaserFiche Client is a different version from the LaserFiche Server. Resolution Upgrade the Client to the same version as the LaserFiche Server or upgrade the Server to the same version as the Client. Workaround Note: This workaround is intended to allow a LaserFiche 4.3 Client to connect to a LaserFiche 5.03 Server. This workaround does not work with LaserFiche 6 If you are using an updated Server and an older Client and cannot upgrade to the newest Client, try the following workaround. To configure the LaserFiche Server to allow legacy clients: From a text editor, open the LFS.ini file. This file can be found in the C:\Windows directory on the computer hosting the LaserFiche Server. Under the [Options] section, add the following line: LegacyClient=1 Note: Some features that are available with a new Client version will not be accessible with the old Client. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Features for Laserfiche Web Import Services 9.0.2.
rows.detailTitle: List of Features for Laserfiche Web Import Services 9.0.2.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Features for Laserfiche Web Import Services 9.0.2. Product: Web Access 9.0.2 KB: 1013206 Published: April 22, 2013 Last Updated: April 22, 2013 Summary This article highlights features for the DocuSign and Laser App Web Import Services. DocuSign Integration Features Configure DocuSign Connect settings to publish information to the Laserfiche DocuSign web service. Directly send existing Laserfiche documents to DocuSign for signatures through Web Access 9.0.2. With version control, signed documents are saved as new versions of the original document. Automatically create Laserfiche documents from signed DocuSign documents. Set Laserfiche field values based on DocuSign data. The integration can set different fields based on whether the document was signed, declined, or voided. Administrators can receive e-mail notifications if there is a communications error with DocuSign or Laserfiche Server. Laser App Integration Features Laser App users can configure Laser App Enterprise to send forms to the Laserfiche Laser App web service. The web service will automatically create Laserfiche documents from Laser App forms. Laser App users can receive a confirmation page with URL links to Web Access of the newly created document. Set Laserfiche field values based on form data. Related Links 1013207 Release Notes for Laserfiche Import Web Services. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Both updated versions of LFSO80.dll and LFSO81.dll resolve the following issue: When a custom integration clones a... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012701/list-of-changes-for-lfso80-and-lf ...
rows.title: List of Changes for LFSO80 and LFSO81 Hotfix 1012701 (November 2010).
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for LFSO80 and LFSO81 Hotfix 1012701 (November 2010).
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for LFSO80 and LFSO81 Hotfix 1012701 (November 2010). Product: Laserfiche 8, SDK 8 KB: 1012701 Published: November 5, 2010 Last Updated: February 28, 2011 Summary This article details hotfix 1012701 for Laserfiche 8.1.2. The fix includes updated versions of LFSO80.dll (version 8.0.2.900) and LFSO81.dll (version 8.1.2.726) that resolve potential issues that may affect custom integrations built using LFSO. Both updated versions of LFSO80.dll and LFSO81.dll resolve the following issue: When a custom integration clones a Client connection, the cloned connection may not properly close if the custom integration remains open for a few minutes after the Laserfiche Client is closed. This can result in licenses not being freed. (64483) The updated version of LFSO81.dll also resolves the following issue: A potential memory leak can cause a "bad allocation" error in certain custom integrations that iterate through a large number entries using the LFFolder.GetChildren() method. (64991) Resolution Updates to LFSO require that the updated version of LFSO be accessible to the desired client program or integration. Download the appropriate version of LFSO and replace the existing copy on the desired workstations. To update LFSO81.dll Click one of the following links to download a zip file with the updated version of LFSO81.dll. KB1012701_win32.zip KB1012701_x64.zip Replace your existing version of LFSO81.dll with the updated version. By default, LFSO81.dll is located at "C:\Program Files\Common Files\Laserfiche\LFObjects." On a 64-bit version of Windows, the 32-bit version of LFSO81.dll is located at "C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\LFObjects." To update LFSO80.dll Click one of the following to download a zip file with the updated version of LFSO80.dll. KB1012701_LFSO80_win32 KB1012701_LFSO80_x64.zip Replace your existing version of LFSO80.dll with the updated version. By default, LFSO80.dll is located at "C:\Program Files\Common Files\Laserfiche\LFObjects." On a 64-bit version of Windows, the 32-bit version of LFSO80.dll is located at "C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\LFObjects." 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: Release Notes for Workflow 10.4
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Workflow 10.4 Product: Workflow Suite KB: 1014015 Published: December 18, 2018 Last Updated: December 18, 2018 Summary Laserfiche Workflow 10.4 includes various feature improvements and bug fixes. For a detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1014014. System Requirements Supported Operating Systems Windows 10 Windows 8.1 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2012 Windows 7 (Service Pack 1) Windows Server 2008 R2 (Service Pack 1) Windows Server 2008 (Service Pack 2) Windows Server 2016 Note: 32-bit and 64-bit versions supported for all Workflow 10 and higher components. Note: If you are running Windows 8 or Windows Server 2012, you must install .NET Framework 4.5, 4.6, or 4.7 before installing Workflow. Requirements for the Workflow Designer Minimum: CPU 1GHz, 512MB RAM. Recommended: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM or higher. Requirements for the Workflow Server and Subscriber Minimum: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM. Recommended: 2, 4, 6, or 8-core CPU, based on need. 4GB RAM or higher. More CPU cores allow more workflows to run simultaneously. Software Requirements Windows Components Windows Message Queuing component: This component must be manually installed on the machine(s) hosting the Workflow Server and Subscriber. Microsoft .NET Framework 4.5, 4.6, or 4.7: This component will automatically be installed during the Workflow installation if it is not installed. Windows Components Necessary for the Workflow Web Service IIS (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: You must install IIS 7 or higher. IIS Basic Authentication and Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. ASP.NET: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: If you are running Windows 8 or Windows Server 2012, you must install the ASP.NET 4.5 feature in IIS. On Windows 10 or Windows Server 2016, the feature may be listed as ASP.NET 4.6 or 4.7. Windows Components for Workflow Web IIS 7 or higher (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting Laserfiche Workflow Web. IIS Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web. ASP.NET 4.5, 4.6, or 4.7: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting Workflow Web. Web Browser: Internet Explorer 9 or later (Internet Explorer 10 or later recommended), Firefox 24, Chrome 30, Safari Supported Database Management Systems SQL Server 2008 SQL Server 2008 R2 SQL Server 2012 SQL Server 2014 SQL Server 2016 SQL Server 2017 Oracle 10g (10.2.0.4+) Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+) Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+) and higher Oracle 12c Note: Express editions of the SQL Server versions listed above are also supprted but not recommended for high-volume Workflow installations. Note: If you are using an Oracle DBMS, the Oracle Client 11.2.0.2.1 with the corresponding version of Oracle Data Access Components (ODAC) must be installed on the machine hosting the Workflow Server. Note: If you are using Oracle 12c, please install the 11.2.0.3 Oracle Client on the machine hosting the Workflow Server. Laserfiche Components Laserfiche Server 8.0 or higher (Laserfiche Server 8.3.1 or higher is recommended.) Note: To use all aspects of the business process feature, you must have Laserfiche Server 9.0 or higher and the Laserfiche Client 9.0 and/or Web Access 9.0 or higher and/or Web Client. Component Known Issue Workflow Installation If you upgrade from Workflow 8.3.2 to Workflow 9.0.x and then immediately upgrade to Workflow 9.1 or later without first using the Workflow 9.0.x configuration manager to configure Workflow, the Workflow 9.1 or later upgrade may not retain your Workflow Server settings from Workflow 8.3.2. You can upgrade from Workflow 8.3.2 to Workflow 9.1 or later. You do not need to do an intermediate upgrade to Workflow 9.0.x. (110343) Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, additional Microsoft Message Queuing configuration may be required. See the Workflow 9.1 Installation Guide for specific instructions. (78523) Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, you may need to provide domain authentication instead of using Local System Account when configuring the Workflow Subscriber. (77933) Workflow Installation When using the Repair option to restore assemblies that were deleted from the GAC, the process may fail. Repairing it a second time will succeed. (70389) Workflow Installation When upgrading to Workflow 9 or later, if the Workflow 8.3 uninstall process fails, it must be manually uninstalled by running the original installer. (95866) Workflow Installation Uninstalling Workflow does not deactivate your license. Prior to uninstalli
